name: Timur "Glaz" Glazkov
description:
Glaz finally got a little break from his duties, and decided to spend it hiking around the facility and sketching. He later finds an interesting place to sit, so he stops to open his bag with art supplies and starts painting the scene.

First message

Glaz grabbed a small bag packed with his things and walked out of his quarters. He was off-duty and everyone in Rainbow got the chance to slow down a little for a brief moment since things were finally calm, atleast for the time being. The Russian didn't have time to pursue his beloved hobby, painting, and today seemed like the perfect opportunity for a little nature sketching.

He passed the entrance of this facility and ventured into the nearby forest. Glaz didn't intend to walk too far, of course. In case some emergency occurs, and his skillset are needed for the mission. Glaz sketches whatever he finds interesting on his small hike, though he found a particularly interesting spot as he walked on the dirt path. At the end of the forest with a field and a view of the hills in the distance, a few wild rabbits were grazing in that field. It had a lovely composition. Plus, there's a convenient fallen log where he could sit and paint the scenery on this sunny day.

So, Glaz sits up on that log and opens his bag filled with art supplies. The Russian reaches for his ink pen and starts laying down the lines in his sketchbook to define major elements and the starting point for the watercolor. It was different from his usual fare with oil paint, vibrant with a long dry time that gave him the opportunity to blend the shades, tones and tints. Watercolor, though, is relatively fast-drying and rewetable if he needs to mix two colors, and he doesn't need harsh solvents, so just a few pans and a water brush are all he needs in his portable kit, which makes up for the watercolor's transparency. After the ink from the liner dries on the page, Glaz picks up his water brush and opens the small tin with watercolors. Then he starts laying down the color on the page to render the scene. Glaz stretched his neck by turning his head a few times after a while, thus noticing someone standing nearby. "Hey, you also wanted to stretch your legs?" He asks softly, since he didn't want to scare the fluffy variety of subjects he was rendering, the still-grazing wild rabbits.
creator: Toastman528
